title: Preparing for Production
description: Configure app icons, splash screens, and app.json for release.

slides.title: From Development to Production 🚀
slides.content:
# The Final Steps

Before publishing your app, you need to polish it and configure metadata. This includes:

- **App icon** – appears on the home screen.
- **Splash screen** – shown while the app loads.
- **App name, version, bundle identifier** – in `app.json`.
- **Permissions** – ensure they are described clearly.

All these are configured in `app.json` (or `app.config.js`) when using Expo.

slides.title: Configuring Icons and Splash Screen

quizData.question: Which file do you modify to set the app icon and splash screen in an Expo project?
quizData.explanation: App icon and splash screen are configured in the `expo` section of `app.json`.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use `expo prebuild` to generate native folders if you need to customise native code. But for most apps, managed workflow is sufficient.
